title: RabbitMQ 4.3 Retries, Priorities, and Consumer Timeouts · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible a Go client and broker-policy compatibility record with one stale generation, blocked notification, goroutine leak, acknowledgement, route, or effect defect; locate cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Go client and broker-policy compatibility record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Compare native delayed retries with TTL and DLX cycles by rewrite cost, loss and duplicate windows, head-of-line effects, topology, and version support; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum 32 strict priority levels with bounded use, starvation evidence, prefetch interaction, and per-priority monitoring; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feature Version Gate
steps.instruction:
Order the Feature Version Gate RabbitMQ and Go messaging evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feature Version Gate.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Feature Version Gate assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feature Version Gate, keep Feature Version Gate connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go messaging team handles case 1521 during bounded worker pool during a dependency slowdown. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce RabbitMQ 4.3 delayed retry, strict priority, and consumer-timeout policy limits; keep credentials and connection URLs out of output; then lower the byte and goroutine budget and preserve rejection evidence. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate RabbitMQ 4.3 behavior on deployed broker version, queue type, policy, feature state, amqp091-go behavior, migration state, and tested fallback.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Feature Version Gate has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

steps.instruction:
